title: Recognizing Warning Signs of a Heart Attack in Older Adults
summary: Heart attack symptoms in older adults, especially women, often look different than the "classic" chest-clutching image — which is part of why they're sometimes missed.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Recognizing Warning Signs of a Heart Attack in Older Adults | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Heart attack symptoms in older adults, especially women, often look different than the "classic" chest-clutching image — which is part of why they're somet
whatYouNeedToKnow: Chest pain is common but not universal, especially in older adults and women.
Shortness of breath, fatigue, and nausea can be the primary symptoms.
Any suspected heart attack is a call-911 emergency, not a wait-and-see situation.

paragraphs: Classic chest pain is a well-known symptom, but older adults — and women in particular — more often experience shortness of breath, unusual fatigue, nausea, or discomfort in the jaw, back, or arm, sometimes without prominent chest pain at all.

This is exactly why any combination of these symptoms, especially if sudden, should be treated as an emergency — call 911 rather than driving them yourself, since emergency responders can begin treatment immediately.

nextSteps: Learn the fuller symptom list, not just chest pain.
Keep emergency numbers and their medication list somewhere immediately accessible.
Never wait to "see if it passes" — call 911 for suspected heart attack symptoms.
disclaimer: If you suspect a heart attack is happening now, call 911 immediately — this article is for general awareness, not real-time emergency guidance.
